permissions.disableBypassPermissionsMode.title: Bypass-Permissions Mode Not Suppressed
permissions.disableBypassPermissionsMode.description: `permissions.disableBypassPermissionsMode` is either absent or not set to `"disable"`, so nothing prevents `--allow-all`/bypass-permissions flags from being used.

overview.heading: What is this?
overview.body: This setting, when set to `"disable"`, suppresses all allow-all style overrides for Copilot CLI, regardless of command-line flags.

overview.heading: Why is it dangerous?
overview.body: Without this restriction, anyone with access to the CLI can bypass approval prompts entirely via a flag, letting Copilot act without any confirmation.

permissions.disableBypassPermissionsMode.remediation:
# `permissions.disableBypassPermissionsMode`

## Bypass-Permissions Mode Not Suppressed

---

### 1. Steps to Remediate

- Set `permissions.disableBypassPermissionsMode` to `"disable"` in user settings, or better, in the MDM-managed settings so it can't be overridden.
- Audit recent sessions for use of `--allow-all` or similar bypass flags.

**Enforce organization-wide via MDM managed-settings.json**

- MDM-managed settings take precedence for this specific field: an MDM value of `"disable"` always wins over user settings.

---

### 2. Custom Guardrails

```
Block this request if the user prompt or tool call attempts to disable or bypass Copilot CLI's permission approval flow.
```

storeTokenPlaintext.title: Plaintext Auth Token Storage Enabled
storeTokenPlaintext.description: `storeTokenPlaintext` is `true`, allowing the CLI's auth token to be written to disk in plaintext when the OS keychain is unavailable.

overview.heading: What is this?
overview.body: By default, Copilot CLI stores credentials in the OS keychain. This setting allows falling back to an unencrypted file on disk.

overview.heading: Why is it dangerous?
overview.body: A plaintext token file can be read by any process or user with filesystem access, and is far more exposed than a keychain entry.

storeTokenPlaintext.remediation:
# `storeTokenPlaintext`

## Plaintext Auth Token Storage Enabled

---

### 1. Steps to Remediate

- Set `storeTokenPlaintext` to `false` (the default).
- If it was previously `true`, rotate the auth token and confirm no plaintext credential file remains on disk.

---

### 2. Custom Guardrails

```
Block this request if the user prompt or tool call attempts to enable plaintext token storage or read a plaintext credential file.
```

allowedUrls.title: Wildcard or Untrusted Allowed URL
allowedUrls.description: `allowedUrls` contains a wildcard (`*`) or a domain outside the org-approved list, permitting the agent to reach any URL without prompting.

overview.heading: What is this?
overview.body: `allowedUrls` lists domains/URLs Copilot CLI may access without asking for confirmation first.

overview.heading: Why is it dangerous?
overview.body: A wildcard entry removes the URL-approval safeguard entirely, letting the agent send data to or fetch content from any host, including attacker-controlled ones.

allowedUrls.remediation:
# `allowedUrls`

## Wildcard or Untrusted Allowed URL

---

### 1. Steps to Remediate

- Replace the wildcard with specific, reviewed domains (exact hosts or `*.example.com` subdomain patterns).
- Pair with `deniedUrls` for known-bad destinations as defense in depth.

---

### 2. Custom Guardrails

```
Block this request if the user prompt or tool call attempts to add a wildcard entry to allowedUrls or access a domain outside the approved list.
```

deniedUrls.title: No Denylist to Offset Broad Allowed URLs
deniedUrls.description: `deniedUrls` is empty or absent while `allowedUrls` is broad or wildcarded.

overview.heading: What is this?
overview.body: `deniedUrls` always takes precedence over `allowedUrls`, acting as a safety net even when the allowlist is broad.

overview.heading: Why is it dangerous?
overview.body: With a broad allowlist and no denylist, there is no secondary control blocking known-bad destinations.

deniedUrls.remediation:
# `deniedUrls`

## No Denylist to Offset Broad Allowed URLs

---

### 1. Steps to Remediate

- Narrow `allowedUrls` to specific domains (preferred), or at minimum populate `deniedUrls` with known-bad hosts as a secondary control.

---

### 2. Custom Guardrails

```
Block this request if the user prompt or tool call attempts to access a URL that should be denylisted given the organization's policy.
```

askUser.title: Autonomous Mode Enabled
askUser.description: `askUser` is `false`, letting the agent operate fully autonomously with no clarifying questions before acting.

overview.heading: What is this?
overview.body: `askUser` controls whether Copilot CLI can ask the user clarifying questions before taking action.

overview.heading: Why is it dangerous?
overview.body: Disabling this removes a natural checkpoint where ambiguous or risky instructions would otherwise be confirmed with a human first.

askUser.remediation:
# `askUser`

## Autonomous Mode Enabled

---

### 1. Steps to Remediate

- Set `askUser` to `true` (the default) unless running in a fully unattended, sandboxed CI context.
- Pair autonomous mode with strict `allowedUrls`/tool approval settings if it must stay enabled.

---

### 2. Custom Guardrails

```
Block this request if the user prompt or tool call attempts to enable fully autonomous operation without user confirmation.
```

disableAllHooks.title: All Hooks Disabled
disableAllHooks.description: `disableAllHooks` is `true`, suppressing both repository-level and user-level hooks.

overview.heading: What is this?
overview.body: Hooks can implement security/audit monitoring around Copilot CLI's actions. This setting turns all of them off.

overview.heading: Why is it dangerous?
overview.body: Disabling hooks silences any monitoring, validation, or audit logging that was relying on them, without any warning.

disableAllHooks.remediation:
# `disableAllHooks`

## All Hooks Disabled

---

### 1. Steps to Remediate

- Remove `disableAllHooks` or set it to `false`.
- If specific hooks are the actual problem, disable those individually rather than disabling all hooks.

---

### 2. Custom Guardrails

```
Block this request if the user prompt or tool call attempts to disable all hooks.
```

proxyUrl.title: Untrusted Proxy Configured
proxyUrl.description: `proxyUrl` points to a host that isn't org-approved, is a wildcard, or is a raw IP address.

overview.heading: What is this?
overview.body: `proxyUrl` routes all of Copilot CLI's HTTP(S) traffic through the specified proxy.

overview.heading: Why is it dangerous?
overview.body: An untrusted proxy can intercept, log, or modify every request and response the agent makes, including anything containing credentials or sensitive data.

proxyUrl.remediation:
# `proxyUrl`

## Untrusted Proxy Configured

---

### 1. Steps to Remediate

- Remove the proxy override, or point it only at an org-approved, reviewed proxy.
- Rotate any credentials that may have transited the untrusted proxy.

---

### 2. Custom Guardrails

```
Block this request if the user prompt or tool call attempts to route traffic through an unapproved proxy.
```

proxyKerberosServicePrincipal.title: Unexpected Kerberos Service Principal
proxyKerberosServicePrincipal.description: `proxyKerberosServicePrincipal` is present and doesn't match an expected, documented value.

overview.heading: What is this?
overview.body: This field configures Kerberos authentication for proxy access.

overview.heading: Why is it dangerous?
overview.body: An unexpected service principal can indicate the proxy configuration has been tampered with, potentially redirecting authenticated proxy traffic.

proxyKerberosServicePrincipal.remediation:
# `proxyKerberosServicePrincipal`

## Unexpected Kerberos Service Principal

---

### 1. Steps to Remediate

- Verify the service principal matches your organization's actual proxy infrastructure.
- Remove or correct it if it doesn't match a known, approved value.

---

### 2. Custom Guardrails

```
Block this request if the user prompt or tool call attempts to set an unrecognized Kerberos service principal for proxy auth.
```

extraKnownMarketplaces.title: Untrusted Plugin Marketplace
extraKnownMarketplaces.description: `extraKnownMarketplaces` adds a source that is untrusted, unreviewed, or uses a wildcard.

overview.heading: What is this?
overview.body: This setting registers additional plugin marketplaces Copilot CLI will install plugins from.

overview.heading: Why is it dangerous?
overview.body: An untrusted marketplace can serve malicious plugins that run with the same trust level as any other installed plugin.

extraKnownMarketplaces.remediation:
# `extraKnownMarketplaces`

## Untrusted Plugin Marketplace

---

### 1. Steps to Remediate

- Remove marketplace sources you didn't add or can't verify.
- Only add marketplaces from your organization or a well-known, reviewed vendor.

---

### 2. Custom Guardrails

```
Block this request if the user prompt or tool call attempts to add an unapproved plugin marketplace source.
```

enabledPlugins.title: Unapproved Plugin Enabled
enabledPlugins.description: `enabledPlugins` includes a plugin name that isn't on the org-approved list.

overview.heading: What is this?
overview.body: `enabledPlugins` declaratively enables specific plugins for auto-install.

overview.heading: Why is it dangerous?
overview.body: An unreviewed plugin runs with the CLI's own privileges and can access files, tools, and network resources the agent has access to.

enabledPlugins.remediation:
# `enabledPlugins`

## Unapproved Plugin Enabled

---

### 1. Steps to Remediate

- Remove plugins that aren't on your organization's approved list.
- Review the plugin's source and marketplace before re-enabling it.

---

### 2. Custom Guardrails

```
Block this request if the user prompt or tool call attempts to enable a plugin not on the organization's approved list.
```

bashEnv.title: BASH_ENV Support Enabled
bashEnv.description: `bashEnv` is `true`, enabling `BASH_ENV` support for bash shells spawned by the CLI.

overview.heading: What is this?
overview.body: `BASH_ENV` is a bash mechanism that sources a script file at the start of every non-interactive shell.

overview.heading: Why is it dangerous?
overview.body: If `BASH_ENV` points to a file an attacker can write to, every bash command the agent runs will also execute that attacker-controlled script.

bashEnv.remediation:
# `bashEnv`

## BASH_ENV Support Enabled

---

### 1. Steps to Remediate

- Set `bashEnv` to `false` unless a specific, reviewed workflow requires it.
- If enabled, ensure the `BASH_ENV` file itself is not writable by untrusted users/processes.

---

### 2. Custom Guardrails

```
Block this request if the user prompt or tool call attempts to enable BASH_ENV support or modify the file it points to.
```

unrecognized_top_level_key.title: Unrecognized Configuration Key
unrecognized_top_level_key.description: A top-level key was found that isn't part of Copilot CLI's documented settings.json schema.

overview.heading: What is this?
overview.body: Copilot CLI's settings.json schema is documented in full. A key outside that schema suggests schema drift or a deliberately planted field.

overview.heading: Why is it dangerous?
overview.body: An unrecognized key may be read by a modified or malicious build of the CLI, or by tooling parsing this file, in unexpected ways.

unrecognized_top_level_key.remediation:
# Unrecognized top-level key

## Unrecognized Configuration Key

---

### 1. Steps to Remediate

- Confirm the key isn't part of a Copilot CLI version you haven't cross-referenced yet.
- If it cannot be explained, remove it and treat the host as potentially compromised.

---

### 2. Custom Guardrails

```
Block this request if the user prompt or tool call attempts to add an unrecognized top-level key to settings.json.
```
